```{r organize_hover_data, cache=F, fig.width = 8}
    stone_time_hover = case_when(
      stoneposition == "right" ~ hover_time_right,
      stoneposition == "middle" ~ hover_time_middle,
      stoneposition == "left" ~ hover_time_left,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    gem_frame_hover = case_when(
      correct_choice_position_part1 == 'left' ~ frames_hover_left,
      correct_choice_position_part1 == 'middle' ~ frames_hover_middle,
      correct_choice_position_part1 == 'right' ~ frames_hover_right
    ),
    gem_time_hover = case_when(
      correct_choice_position_part1 == 'left' ~ hover_time_left,
      correct_choice_position_part1 == "middle" ~ hover_time_middle,
      correct_choice_position_part1 == 'right' ~ hover_time_right
    ),
    other_frame_hover = case_when(
      stoneposition == "left" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "middle" ~ frames_hover_right,
      stoneposition == "left" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "right" ~ frames_hover_middle,
      stoneposition == "middle" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "right" ~ frames_hover_left,
      stoneposition == "middle" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "left" ~ frames_hover_right,
      stoneposition == "right" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "middle" ~ frames_hover_left,
      stoneposition == "right" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "left" ~ frames_hover_middle,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    other_time_hover = case_when(
      stoneposition == "left" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "middle" ~ hover_time_right,
      stoneposition == "left" & correct_choice_position_part1== "right" ~ hover_time_middle,
      stoneposition == "middle" & correct_choice_position_part1 == "right" ~ hover_time_left,
      stoneposition == "middle" & correct_choice_position_part1== "left" ~ hover_time_right,
      stoneposition == "right" & correct_choice_position_part1== "middle" ~ hover_time_left,
      stoneposition == "right" & correct_choice_position_part1== "left" ~ hover_time_middle,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    
  )
  




```

```{r pstones_diff, cache=F}

exp2_pstones_diff <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial) %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(
    pstone_p1 = mean(chose_onlystones),
    pstone_p2 = mean(chose_onlystones_p2)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pstone_diff = pstone_p2 - pstone_p1
  )

t.test(exp2_pstones_diff$pstone_diff)

```

Exp. 2 within-subject t-test, pstones part 2 - pstones part 1 `r apa_print(t.test(exp2_pstones_diff$pstone_diff))$statistic`

```{r estimate_means, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 3}
library(patchwork) 
exp2_chose_gems <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>% 
  summarise(pgem = mean(chose_gems))

exp2_estimate <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(pgem = first(number_correct_part1)/50)

exp2_chose_gems_p2 <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(pgem = mean(chose_gems_p2))

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext) 

# Combine experiments (same as before)
combined <- bind_rows(
  exp2_chose_gems %>% mutate(exp = "Part 1"),
  exp2_chose_gems_p2 %>% mutate(exp = "Part 2"),
  exp2_estimate %>% mutate(exp = "Part 1 Estimate")
) %>%
  mutate(exp = factor(exp, levels = c("Part 1", "Part 2", "Part 1 Estimate")))
  

# Compute mean ± SE
summary_df <- combined %>%
  group_by(exp) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pgem = mean(pgem, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_pgem   = sd(pgem, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot: diamond for mean, error bars for SE

# create annotation df
asterisk_df_pstones <- summary_df %>%
  mutate(
    label = "***",
    y = mean_pgem + se_pgem + 0.01   # vertical offset above error bar
  )




exp2_estimate <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(pgem = first(number_correct_part1)/50)

exp2_chose_gems <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(pgem = mean(chose_gems, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

exp2_chose_gems_p2 <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(pgem = mean(chose_gems_p2, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

p1_vs_estimate <- exp2_estimate %>%
  mutate(pgem_estimate = pgem) %>%
  select(subj_id, pgem_estimate) %>%
  inner_join(
    exp2_chose_gems,
    by = "subj_id"
  )

p2_vs_estimate <- exp2_estimate %>%
  mutate(pgem_estimate = pgem) %>%
  select(subj_id, pgem_estimate) %>%
  inner_join(
    exp2_chose_gems_p2,
    by = "subj_id"
  )

p1_vs_p2 <- exp2_chose_gems_p2 %>%
  mutate(pgem_p2 = pgem) %>%
  select(subj_id, pgem_p2) %>%
  inner_join(
    exp2_chose_gems,
    by = "subj_id"
  )

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return(NA_character_)
  if (p < 0.001) return("***")
  if (p < 0.01) return ("**")
  if (p < 0.05) return ("*")
  sprintf("p = %.2f", p)
}

p1_p2_ttest <- fmt_p(t.test(p1_vs_p2$pgem_p2, p1_vs_p2$pgem, paired = TRUE)$p.value)
p1_estimate <- fmt_p(t.test(p1_vs_estimate$pgem_estimate, p1_vs_estimate$pgem, paired = TRUE)$p.value)
p2_estimate <- fmt_p(t.test(p2_vs_estimate$pgem_estimate, p2_vs_estimate$pgem, paired = TRUE)$p.value)


# --- define x positions explicitly ---
x_p1       <- which(levels(summary_df$exp) == "Part 1")
x_p2       <- which(levels(summary_df$exp) == "Part 2")
x_estimate <- which(levels(summary_df$exp) == "Part 1 Estimate")

# 2) choose y positions slightly above the plotted range (adjust if needed)
y_top <- max(summary_df$mean_pgem + summary_df$se_pgem, na.rm = TRUE)
pad   <- diff(range(c(0.35, 0.45))) * 0.06   # small fraction of y-range
# --- Revised bracket code: place two brackets at y = 0.35 and one at y = 0.45 with downward caps --- 
# --- fixed bracket block: define seg_df_pgem first, then compute seg_up/seg_down yend columns ---

# explicit y positions you requested
y_p1_p2  <- 0.365   # p1 vs p2
y_p1_est <- 0.4375   # p1 vs estimate (caps facing down)
y_p2_est <- 0.355   # p2 vs estimate

# geometry params
cap_height <- 0.005
line_w     <- 0.6
text_pad   <- 0.006
text_size  <- 5

# 1) define seg_df_pgem (must exist before creating seg_up/seg_down)
seg_df_pgem <- tibble::tibble(
  x       = c(x_p1, x_p1,  x_p2),
  xend    = c(x_p2, x_estimate, x_estimate),
  y       = c(y_p1_p2, y_p1_est, y_p2_est),
  label   = c(p1_p2_ttest, p1_estimate, p2_estimate),
  cap_dir = c(1, -1, 1)   # 1 = caps upward, -1 = caps downward
)

# 2) create seg_up and seg_down and precompute yend (so aes() has all required columns)
seg_up <- seg_df_pgem %>%
  dplyr::filter(cap_dir == 1) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yend = y + cap_height)

seg_down <- seg_df_pgem %>%
  dplyr::filter(cap_dir == -1) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yend = y - cap_height)

# 3) compute text positions
text_df_pgem <- seg_df_pgem %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x = (x + xend) / 2,
    y = ifelse(cap_dir == -1, y + text_pad, y - text_pad),
    fontface = ifelse(label %in% c("*", "**", "***"), "bold", "plain")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(x, y, label, fontface)

# ---------------------------
# Recreate base plot (fresh)
# ---------------------------
pgem_estimate <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = exp, y = mean_pgem)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_pgem - se_pgem,
        ymax = mean_pgem + se_pgem),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "black", color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0.33, 0.45),
    breaks = seq(0.1, 1.1, by = 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "P(*gems*)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_markdown(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_markdown(size = 14),
        axis.title.x = element_markdown(size = 14),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12))

# ---------------------------
# Verified bracket dataframes:
# seg_df_pgem, seg_up, seg_down, text_df_pgem
# (from your validated code above)
# ---------------------------

# Add horizontal bracket lines (these use the validated objects you already printed)
pgem_estimate <- pgem_estimate +
  geom_segment(
    data = seg_df_pgem,
    mapping = aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = line_w,
    lineend = "butt"
  )

if (nrow(seg_up) > 0) {
  pgem_estimate <- pgem_estimate +
    geom_segment(
      data = seg_up,
      mapping = aes(x = x, xend = x, y = y, yend = yend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = line_w
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = seg_up,
      mapping = aes(x = xend, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = line_w
    )
}

if (nrow(seg_down) > 0) {
  pgem_estimate <- pgem_estimate +
    geom_segment(
      data = seg_down,
      mapping = aes(x = x, xend = x, y = y, yend = yend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = line_w
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = seg_down,
      mapping = aes(x = xend, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = line_w
    )
}

pgem_estimate <- pgem_estimate +
  geom_text(
    data = text_df_pgem,
    mapping = aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, fontface = fontface),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = text_size,
    vjust = 0.5
  )

# display
print(pgem_estimate)

ggsave(
  "pgem_estimate.png",
  pgem_estimate,
  width = 8,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

```

Difference in means for part 1 p(gems) and part 2 p(gems): `r apa_print(t.test(p1_vs_p2$pgem_p2, p1_vs_p2$pgem, paired = TRUE))$statistic`

Difference in means for part 1 p(gems) and part 1 p(gems) estimate: `r apa_print(t.test(p1_vs_estimate$pgem_estimate, p1_vs_estimate$pgem, paired = TRUE))$statistic`

Difference in means for part 1 p(gems) estimate and part 2 p(gems): `r apa_print(t.test(p2_vs_estimate$pgem_estimate, p2_vs_estimate$pgem, paired = TRUE))$statistic`


```{r conf_analysis, cache=F}

correct_conf_summary <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial, is_consistent) %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(mean_conf_subj = mean(confidence, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_conf = mean(mean_conf_subj),
    se_conf = sd(mean_conf_subj)/sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  ) 

incorrect_conf_summary <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial, !is_consistent) %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(mean_conf_subj = mean(confidence, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_conf = mean(mean_conf_subj),
    se_conf = sd(mean_conf_subj)/sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  ) 


gems_stones_conf <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial, chose_gems, !is_consistent)%>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(mean_conf_subj = mean(confidence)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_conf = mean(mean_conf_subj),
    se_conf = sd(mean_conf_subj)/sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  )

stones_gems_conf <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>% 
  filter(!is_catch_trial, !chose_gems, chose_gems_p2) %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(mean_conf_subj = mean(confidence)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_conf = mean(mean_conf_subj),
    se_conf = sd(mean_conf_subj)/sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  )

stones_stones_conf <- two_parts_df_exp2 %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial, !chose_gems, !chose_gems_p2, !is_consistent) %>%
  group_by(subj_id) %>%
  summarise(mean_conf_subj = mean(confidence)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_conf = mean(mean_conf_subj),
    se_conf = sd(mean_conf_subj)/sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  )



```

(all part 2 incorrect trials)
gems -> stones mean confidence `r printnum(gems_stones_conf$mean_conf)`, SE = `r printnum(gems_stones_conf$se_conf)`

stones -> gems mean confidence `r printnum(stones_gems_conf$mean_conf)`, SE = `r printnum(stones_gems_conf$se_conf)`

stones -> stones mean confidence `r printnum(stones_stones_conf$mean_conf)`, SE = `r printnum(stones_stones_conf$se_conf)`

```{r gems, cache=F}
library(lme4)

gems_data <- phase_2_df %>%
  filter(!is_catch_trial) %>%
  inner_join(
    two_parts_df_exp2 %>% 
      filter(!is_catch_trial) %>%
      mutate(chose_gems_p1 = chose_gems) %>%
      select(chose_gems_p1, subj_id, trial_num),
    by = c("subj_id", "trial_num")
  ) %>%
  filter(!chose_gems_p1, !is_consistent)

model <- glmer(
  chose_gems ~ 1 + (1 | subj_id),
  data = gems_data,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(
    optimizer = "bobyqa",)
)

summary(model)

plogis(fixef(model)[1])

```

We ran a mixed-effects logistic regression model, for the probability that participants chose gems in part 2 given that they did not choose gems in part 1 and incorrectly reconstruct in part 2. Participants choose the option with gems with a probability of `r printnum(round(plogis(fixef(model)[1]),2))`, significantly higher than chance (β = `r printnum(round(fixef(model)[1], 2))`,  
SE = `r printnum(round(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "Std. Error"], 2))`, 
*z* = `r printnum(round(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "z value"], 2))`, 
*p* `r printp(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "Pr(>|z|)"])`)


```{r chose_onlystones, cache=F}

two_parts_df_exp1 <-read.csv(".../data/exp1/two_parts_df_exp1.csv")


chose_onlystones_df_combined <- bind_rows(two_parts_df_exp2, two_parts_df_exp1) %>%
  filter(chose_onlystones, !chose_onlystones_p2)



model <- glmer(
  chose_gems_p2 ~ 1 + (1 | subj_id),
  data = chose_onlystones_df_combined,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(
    optimizer = "bobyqa",)
)

summary(model)

plogis(fixef(model)[1])
  

```

Running a mixed-effects logistic regression model, combining data from both Exp. 1 and 2, we find that participants are more likely to choose the option with gems given that they chose the stones only option in part 1 and were incorrect in part 2. The probability of choosing the option with gems is `r printnum(round(plogis(fixef(model)[1]), 2))`. This effect is marginally significant  (β = `r printnum(round(fixef(model)[1], 2))`,  
SE = `r printnum(round(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "Std. Error"], 2))`, 
*z* = `r printnum(round(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "z value"], 2))`, 
*p* `r printp(summary(model)$coefficients[1, "Pr(>|z|)"])`)



\newpage
